title: Community Fairness Configuration v1.0
description: A machine-readable specification for community-defined algorithmic fairness parameters. This schema defines the interchange format for community fairness configurations — any tool that reads or produces this format is interoperable with the Community-Defined Fairness ecosystem.

cdf_version.description: Version of the Community-Defined Fairness specification this config conforms to.

priority_groups.description: Racial/ethnic groups the community identified as requiring elevated scrutiny in the audit. These groups were selected by community participants, not by the auditor.

fairness_target.description: The reference group whose outcome rate sets the fairness benchmark. All other groups' disparate impact ratios are computed relative to this group.

fairness_threshold.description: The minimum acceptable disparate impact ratio. Groups with DI below this threshold are flagged. The EEOC default is 0.80; communities may set this higher or lower.

domain.description: The decision domain this configuration applies to. A community may set different thresholds for different domains.

jurisdiction.description: The geographic or institutional scope this configuration applies to (e.g., 'Detroit, MI', 'State of Michigan', 'Acme Corp hiring').

record_id.description: Unique identifier for this configuration record. Generated at creation time.

input_protocol.description: The method used to collect community input.

input_date.description: ISO 8601 date when the community input session took place.

input_location.description: Where the input session took place.

input_participants.description: Number of community members who participated.

facilitator.description: Name or role of the session facilitator.

facilitator_independent.description: Whether the facilitator is independent of the organization being audited.

demographic_representation.description: Breakdown of participants by racial/ethnic group (e.g., {"Black": 6, "White": 3, "Latinx": 5}).

priority_groups.description: How priority groups were selected (e.g., '50% participant agreement').

reference_group.description: How the reference group was selected (e.g., 'simple majority vote').

threshold.description: How the threshold was determined (e.g., 'median of individual responses').

decision_method.description: Documentation of how each decision was made.

threshold_distribution.description: Distribution of individual threshold responses from participants.

dissent.description: Recorded dissenting positions and how many participants held them.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this configuration was created.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this configuration should be re-elicited. Default: 24 months from input_date.

provenance.description: Provenance record linking this configuration to its community input source. This is what makes an audit 'community-valid' rather than 'standard'.

audit_classification.description: Classification of audits produced using this configuration. 'community_valid' requires full provenance. 'low_confidence' if participants < 10.

custom_metrics.description: Additional fairness metrics the community requested beyond disparate impact.

minimum_evidence_tier.description: Minimum VEF evidence_tier vendors must submit. Submissions below this tier receive INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.

minimum_group_n.description: Minimum non-excluded rows required per group. Default: 30.

absolute_floors.description: Per-outcome, per-group absolute rate floors, such as {"resolution_success": {"AAVE": 0.95}}.

evidence_requirements.description: Evidence quality and sample requirements vendors must satisfy before bias metrics are evaluated.

allow_demo_attestors.description: Whether demo-only trusted keys may create verified VEF verdicts for this standard.

trusted_key_ids.description: Allowed public key IDs by attestation type, or a string array applying to all attestation types.

attestor_policy.description: Role-based source-verification policy for VEF attestations.
